headline: Stony Creek (VIC) Race Day Tips — 2026-03-07
analysis:
At Stony Creek, the Soft 7 track set the stage for the day's races, kicking off with the Melina Bath Mp Maiden Plate over 2100m for 3-year-olds and up. SNOBBERY, who placed last start at this venue on a similar surface, appears well-positioned to perform. MAYEKI, narrowly beaten at long odds previously at Yarra Valley, could also contend with some luck. GOLD FRANC, from a reputable stable, may appeal to those playing wide in exotics despite a seventh-place finish at Pakenham last start. Meanwhile, NOBLE FALCON, returning from a nine-week spell, showed promise in a recent trial, making them an outsider with potential.

In the Meeniyan Hotel After Party Maiden Plate at 1600m, KADDARI commands attention, having placed as a favorite at Werribee and showing consistency with three placings from seven runs this prep. MR MARGOLIN also poses a threat, having secured third in his only start at Pakenham for a strong stable. On debut, GUTHRIE can't be overlooked, while VELVET CITY, after running close at Pakenham, could surprise under the guidance of Nathan McPherson.

The Meeniyan Iga Maiden Plate over 1100m promises competitiveness, with ZOUSOUTH likely to overcome a wide draw due to its speed and astute training. UNA GENTLE, with three placing finishes this prep, remains a significant threat. LE NOTRE, returning from a lengthy spell, has potential, as does the debutant HE'S FIERCE, trained by Michael Huglin.

The Sen Dr Michael Bourke OAM Sprint over 1100m sees JUICE BOX as a strong contender, having led throughout for a decisive win at Sale. TRAK CHILLER, with two consecutive victories at Stony Creek, could challenge if in form. ICHIBERU, with recent placings, offers each-way value, and AUSSIE ADAPTOR, fresh off a maiden breakthrough, is also in the mix.

In the Mary Aldred Mp 0 - 56 Handicap at 1000m, PHANTOM PRINCE stands out following a strong win at odds at Stony Creek. STEWART, with consistent form, shouldn't be underestimated, while IRRESISTIBLE SIR, racing again soon after recent efforts, presents a solid case. DOMINANT MISS has positive trial results to consider after a spell, providing enticing each-way odds.

The Tac Rob Meyer 0 - 56 Handicap over 2100m is highlighted by GINGER SWEET, who recently placed at Pakenham and maintains excellent form. REMIGIUS, consistently in contention as a favorite, is expected to challenge seriously. BAFFLECK and REAL INTRIGUE, both showing recent promise, remain ones to watch for potential upsets.

In the highlight, the Ladbrokes Stony Creek Cup at 2100m, CHASING APHRODITE looks promising with good form returning to non-metro class. STATION ONE, with a strong trial performance, adds intrigue, as does REGAL LION, stepping down from metro grade with a reliable campaign record. CABLE DANCER, also stepping down, still poses a threat from a reputable stable.

The day concludes with the Danny O'Brien Mp 0 - 56 Handicap over 1600m, where HEY BELLA’s recent third at Yarra Valley puts her in a favorable position. AVONVIEW offers some value with consistent placings this prep, while MARIOTA, following a recent trial, provides appealing each-way claims. BEARBRASSBLACKFISH, further back last time, still warrants attention, especially from a Greg Eurell-trained perspective.
meta_description: Expert tips for all races at Stony Creek (VIC) on 2026-03-07.

headline: Storm Set to Capitalise on Weakened Rabbitohs at AAMI Park
analysis: The Melbourne Storm look primed to take advantage of a Rabbitohs side missing two crucial backline players. History is on their side, having won the previous four encounters against Souths at AAMI Park. While it's by no means guaranteed, the odds are attractive at $2.10 for the home team, bolstered by home-ground support. Given the form and venue record, a moderate confidence level of 3/5 suggests a solid, if not certain, value bet.
